page: Microsoft Azure F16als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 server family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server allocates 32.0 GB of system memory, translating to a low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier, reflecting the high clock speed of the AMD EPYC processor. Multi-threaded performance, such as bzip3 decompression, registers in the strong tier, while multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores sit in the average tier. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform within the average range, indicating that while the processor is highly capable, the 32.0 GB memory capacity and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio may limit memory-bound scaling.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F16als_v7 offers a high-compute, low-memory profile. This resource density is optimized for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints, allowing users to avoid paying for unneeded RAM. However, the lack of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data. This server is best suited for workloads such as web serving, single-threaded batch processing, lightweight serialization or compression tasks, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F16als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv7 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by high stress-ng and single-threaded compression benchmark scores. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth sit in the average tier. This low-memory, high-compute profile offers a cost-effective solution for compute-bound workloads that do not demand large memory footprints. It is highly suited for web serving, data compression, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 32 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine designed for single-threaded efficiency and low-memory workloads requiring dedicated processing power.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Configured with 32.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and no local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression benchmark performance
Delivers strong small-model LLM text generation speeds
Optimized for web serving, batch processing, and compute-heavy tasks
